2.-world/humanity/ethnicities/arumahi.ht.title: Arumahi
2.-world/humanity/ethnicities/arumahi.ht.description: The Arumahi are an ethnic group originating from the central region of Threscya. They formed two major civilisations around the Kymerean and Arumotes Rivers, one of which collapsed and was remade into the city of Yvanthryl, and became the heart of the new Union. The Arumotes civilisation however remained a fairly minor settlement within the Union, until after Pralayehena, upon which the local nobility were divided into the two houses of House Hykar and House Genovar, who were given rule of their respective twin city states which the settlement had slowly developed into. The Arumahi make up the largest ethnic proportion within Threscya.

2.-world/humanity/important-events/prala.title: Pralayehena
2.-world/humanity/important-events/prala.description: The day of Pralayehena was the day that the Union was destroyed by a Prydeshah uprising, lead by the Divine and a squadron of Dreadknights, and the kingdom of Threscya was founded.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/hanlyen.title: Hanlyeng
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/hanlyen.description:
Tsashen
Imaren City

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/hanlyen.title: Imaren City
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/hanlyen.description: Imaren City was the previous capital of the Northern Province, currently occupied by Hanlyeng it now functions as a central military hub for the war effort.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/hanlyen.title: Tsashen
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/hanlyen.description: Tsashen is the capital city of Hanlyeng.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/pey-aeh.title: Kehysat
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/pey-aeh.description: Kehysat, the City of Forgemasters, is a city located just next to the base of the Great Drakhon and is the closest livable city to the Supervent.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/pey-aeh.title: Pey Aehtya
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/pey-aeh.description:
Soleche
Kehysat

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/pey-aeh.title: Soleche
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/pey-aeh.description: Soleche is the capital of Pey Aehtya.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/prydesh.title: Prydeshah
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/prydesh.description:
Varadhapuram
Savarna

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/prydesh.title: Savarna
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/prydesh.description: Savarna, the Shining City, is the most populated and largest city in Prydeshah and functions as it's unofficial capital.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/prydesh.title: Varadhapuram
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/prydesh.description: Varadhapuram is the capital city of Prydeshah.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Basin Province
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Basin Province occupies the area north of the Crownlands and east of the Northern Province. Due to it's distance from the Great Drakhon it is also the coldest province. The province is bordered by the Northern Obsidian Mountains at the Upper Basin, which gives the Basin it's name.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Lokyen City
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: Lokyen City is the capital city of the Basin Province.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Central Province
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Central province is located next to the Crownlands at the heart of Threscya. It borders the Southern and Mountain Provinces.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Genovaros
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: Genovaros is the capital city of the Central Province.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Crownlands
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description:
The Crownlands are the heart of the Threscyan Empire, and is a region of the Threscyan Kingdom which is under direct rule by the King. The capital city of the Crownlands is Sel Amaryn.
The southern border of the Crownlands is marked by the Kymerean River which separates it from the Central and |Mountain provinces, and the region ends in the north just before reaching the Basin's capital, Lokyen City.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Holy Citadel
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Holy Citadel is the seat of the Divine and the architectural symbol of his power. A massive sprawl of towering buildings, some up to three millennia old, and at it's heart is the Divine's Cathedral Primos which he resides within. The Holy Citadel is built within the borders of the Achreyon and Kymerean rivers and the Imaren Strait, exactly above the boundaries of the old city of Yvanthryl before it was sunken into the Ignemar Moltar Caves below.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Sel Amaryn
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description:
Sel Amaryn is the Capital of the Threscyan Empire. The city is built within the Crownlands, next to the Imaren Strait at the intersection of the Kymerean and Achreyon rivers.
At the centre of Sel Amaryn, bordered by the two rivers and the strait lies the Holy Citadel which is legally independent from Threscya, as a superior religious authority over the Ters Ilystra and is the seat of the Divine.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Eastern Province
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Eastern Province is located on the far eastern edge of Threscya, bordered by the Mountain Province, and the Eastern section of the Obsidian Mountains.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Ranesa
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: Ranesa is the capital city of the Eastern Province.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Mountain Province
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Mountain Province is located towards the centre of Threscya, bordering the Eastern and Central provinces as well as the Northern Obsidian Mountains.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Orlenyth
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: Orlenyth is the capital city of the Mountain Province.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Fort Petramos
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: Fort Petramos is the current wartime capital of the Northern Province. The previous capital was Imaren City.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Northern Province
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Northern Province originally bordered Hanlyeng at the Sinue River and the Basin to the east, however the Hanlyeng border is currently disputed and has essentially moved halfway eastward across the province. The province's original capital is the currently Hanlen occupied Imaren City.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Hykaros
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: Hykaros is the capital city of the Southern Province.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Southern Province
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Southern Province exists on the Threscyan Lower Peninsula.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Arhyende
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: Arhyende is the capital city of the Western Province.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Western Province
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description: The Western Province occupies the strait-side of Cydon Island, which was originally the Kingdom of Dyzhe.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.title: Threscya
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/threscy.description:
The Threscyan Empire was founded on the day of Pralayehena by the Divine. Threscya specifically refers to the nation of Threscya which is a member state of the empire of the same name, and legally an equivalent peer to other nations such as Prydeshah and Toheren. However in practice, the Threscyan ruling class controls the entire empire and this distinction doesn't actually have any effect.
The kingdom of Threscya comprises of the entire territory of the historic Union as well as some early territories acquired within the Divine's first few years of conquest, including land from the region of Hanlyeng which was added to the Northern Reach, the surviving east side of the Kingdom of Dyzhe which became the Westward Province. Some of the land the Divine took from the newly formed country of Toheren was used to form an ethnostate for the Prydeshah, while the rest was absorbed into the |Highplains. As of the modern day, all countries under the Drakhon have been absorbed into the empire as a whole; despite nations such as Toheren and Pey Aehtya still technically under independent governance they are subservient to Threscyan power and pay taxes to the Divine.
The Holy Citadel is on paper an independent territory from the nation of Threscya, governed directly by God and is the capital of the empire, however since the Threscyan kingdom vs Threscyan empire is non existant in practice, the Citadel are essentially the capital of both Threscya and the empire simultaneously.
The primary ethnicity of Threscya is comprised of the three ethnic groups of three kingdoms which colluded to form the Union: the Arumahi people who lived within the Central Lands, the Ehyros from the Obsidian Mountains and the Nyven of the Basin. Over the years migration has shifted this demographic somewhat. The Capital of Sel Amaryn has the largest Prydeshah population outside the country itself, as there were a few Prydeshah who did not wish to migrate to their new nation in the days after Pralayehena.
The Crownlands
Westward Isle
Southern Peninsula
Central Province
The Highplains
Mountain Province
The Basin
Northern Reach

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/toheren.title: Khadimar
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/toheren.description: Khadimar is the capital city of Toheren.

2.-world/humanity/places/nations/toheren.title: Toheren
2.-world/humanity/places/nations/toheren.description:
Toheren is located at the southeast edge of Ters Ilystra, next to the Eastern Obsidian Mountains and the Labyresa Mountains.
Khadimar

2.-world/humanity/places/z_historic-nati.title: Union
2.-world/humanity/places/z_historic-nati.description:
The Union was founded by a coalition of wealthy and powerful merchants and warlords, some of whom were living through the collapse of an ancient Arumahi kingdom within the Central Lands, though a few were individuals from other neighbouring lands. Since the Union's leadership was not founded on royal bloodlines or kingdoms, many figures of power, including many of these merchants themselves were also Alchemists, as before the development of sophisticated technology or innovation in inkworking, the alchemists posessed an innate labour advantage, that eventually became reinforced by social heirarchies and grew beyond proportion. Soon this class of powerful Alchemists would morph into the Alchemical Council, a political entity which governed the union and was exclusively composed of Alchemists.
The Union was made up of a coalition of kingdoms, both willingly having joined the empire or forced through military might. The Union was comprised of all major kingdoms on the Ilystran Continent, (apart from Hanlyeng in the far north and closer to the day of Pralayehena, the newly formed Toheren). These kingdoms occupied most of the Central Lands (an area made up of the current Threscyan Crownlands, Central Province and Mountain Province) and some small amounts of territory beyond.
Yvanthryl

2.-world/humanity/places/z_historic-nati.title: Yvanthryl
2.-world/humanity/places/z_historic-nati.description: Yvanthryl, also known as the Inkswept Citadel, was the capital city of the Union. The city now remains exactly beneath the Holy Citadel of Sel Amaryn, below ground in the Ignemar Caves.

2.-world/humanity/places/pyrkhamen.html.title: Pyrkhamen
2.-world/humanity/places/pyrkhamen.html.description: The name for this world. The Pyrkhamen is something like a giant stone sponge, where everything is rock up and down. Within this infinite series of rock, there are pockets of Void, which allows for the existance of Ink alongside Moltar, and creates something like an air pocket within the stone. Far enough away from these pockets, the rock turns to pure moltar. These currents of moltar determine the gravitational orientation of each 'pocket' within the Pyrkhamen, and create the gravitational force.

2.-world/humanity/places/ters-dravenyr.h.title: Ters Dravenyr
2.-world/humanity/places/ters-dravenyr.h.description: Translates to "The Savage Lands", a term developed by the Church of the Divine to designate all lands outside the reach of the Divine or outside the Threscyan Empire. It is colloquially used to mean the lands beyond the light of the Great Drakhon.

2.-world/humanity/places/ters-ilystra.ht.title: Ters Ilystra
2.-world/humanity/places/ters-ilystra.ht.description: Translates to "The Enlightened Lands", a term developed by the Church of the Divine to designate all lands inside the territory of the Divine or within the Threscyan Empire. It is colloquially used to mean the lands within the light of the Great Drakhon.

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_minCollapseDepth.description: Only allow outline items to be collapsed if they are at least this many levels deep in the tree.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
